characters.name: Balladbird Lee
characters.description: Balladbird LeeGameAnimeDebutGungraveBiologicalSpeciesSuperiorGenderMaleHair ColorBlackOther InfoAffiliationMillennionOccupationSpyMember ofthe Family'sBig FourFriend(s)Harry MacDowellBob PoundmaxBear WalkenBunji KugashiraBrandon Heat(Formerly)Affilliate(s)Harry MacDowell(Superior; formerly colleague)Bob Poundmax(Colleague)Bear Walken(Colleague)Bunji Kugashira(Colleague; formerly subordinate)Brandon Heat(Colleague; formerly)Big Daddy(Superior; formerly)Enemy(s)Beyond the GraveMika AsagiDr. TWeapon(s)SenbonStatusDeceased Balladbird Lee(バラッドバード・リー,Baraddobādo Rī?)is one of the Big Four inMillennion's Family, a spy for the organization, a major antagonist and boss character in theoriginalGungravegame, and a major antagonist in theanime adaption. He's brought back inGungrave G.O.R.Eby theRaven Clan, and serves as a minor boss in the game. Lee is a punk Balladbird Lee was generally a calm person who values his friends greatly and understands them and their ambitions well. Like Harry, he strives to reach the top of Millennion, although does so in a quieter and less forward way. Harry has said that Lee understands him the best, due to their shared ambition of rising to the top. Lee never capitalized on his own ambition, favoring Harry's for his more expansive vision of the future. Chief in Lee's friendships were his relationships with Bob Poundmax and Harry MacDowell. Lee considered Bob his best friend and was willing to allow Bob to be saved from his cerebral thrombosis via the then-questionable Superiorization, but only after the procedure was successfully performed on Lee first. In order to keep up with Harry's ruthlessness and demands, Lee secretly kills the humans who were physically too weak to be converted to Orgmen in his Superior form. The elderlyScott the Snitchhinted that Lee had killed over two thousand people; whether or not these people were a part of that count is unknown. In Lee's Superior form, he emits a high pitched laugh, a sharp contrast to his calm demeanor as a human.

characters.name: Bear Walken
characters.description: Bear WalkenDebutGungraveBiologicalSpeciesSuperiorGenderMaleHair ColorWhiteOther InfoAffiliationMillennionOccupationSweeperMember of the Family's Big FourFamilySherry MacDowell-Walken(Daughter)Harry MacDowell(Son-in Law)Friend(s)Harry MacDowellBob PoundmaxBalladbird LeeBunji KugashiraAffilliate(s)Harry MacDowell(Superior)Bob Poundmax(Colleague)Balladbird Lee(Colleague)Bunji Kugashira(Colleague)Enemy(s)Beyond the GraveMika AsagiDr. TStatusDeceasedVoice ActorsJapanese VABeau Billingslea(Anime only)English VARyuzaburo Otomo(Anime only) Bear Walken(ベア・ウォーケン,Bea Wōken?)was one of the Big Four inMillennion's the Family, a veteran sweeper for the organization, a major antagonist and boss character in theoriginalGungravegame, and a major antagonist in theanime adaption. Revived by theRaven Clanand returning inGungrave G.O.R.Eas a minor Boss, he's seeking revenge against Grave for the deaths of hisson-in-lawandhis daughter. Bear is calm and collected and handles matters with utmost solemnity. Bear had recognized and distrustedHarryand his eagerness to involve himself in Millennion's affairs. When he was invited to Harry's new house, Bear dismissed Harry's attempted pleasantries outright and instead opted to determine Harry's goals through studying his eyes, claiming that "the eyes never lie". Having detected Harry's drive and willpower to stop at nothing until he gets to the top, Bear approves of Harry as Sherry's husband. This decision ties Bear to Harry, and though Bear inwardly disapproves of Necrolization and Superiorization and Millennion's new Machiavellian policies, he remains with the organization and goes through Superiorization to protect Sherry. Mika further notes from observing Bear that he "doesn't seem like a bad person". Bear is calm and collected and handles matters with utmost solemnity. characters.description: Beyond The GraveG.O.R.EOverdoseGungraveNameKanjiビヨンド・ザ・グレイヴRomanjiBiyondo Za GureivuEnglishBeyond the GraveAliases/NicknamesJapaneseGrave(グレイヴ,,Gureivu?)Gunslinger of ResurrectionDeath God(死神,,Shinigami?)Big Bro(兄貴,,Aniki?)(by Bunji Kugashira)Brandon Heat(ブランドン・ヒート,,Burandon Hīto?)EnglishGraveGunslinger of ResurrectionGrim ReaperBig Bro(by Bunji Kugashira)Brandon HeatProfileGenderMaleAge43 (Original Game)46 (Gungrave: Overdose)59 (Gungrave VR)Unknown (Gungrave: G.O.R.E)SpeciesDeadman/NecrolyzerOther InformationHair ColorPurple-ish black (Gungrave, Gungrave: OD)Black (Gungrave G.O.R.E)Eye ColorAmberWeaponsCerberusLeft Head and Right Head (Pistols)Death Hauler(Coffin)RelationshipsFamilyMika Asagi(Adopted daughter)FriendsBig DaddyMaria AsagiDr. TJyuji KabaneRocketbilly RedcadillacSpike HubieQuartzDr. AsoAffiliatesQuartzDr. AsoMika AsagiBig DaddyMaria AsagiDr. TJyuji KabaneRocketbilly RedcadillacSpike HubieGatou GangEnemiesMillenion(underHarry MacDowell's leadership)Corsione FamilyRaven ClanBunji Kugashira(Rival)Zell CondorbraveStatusStatusAliveBeyond The Grave, or simplyGrave(ビヨンド・ザ・グレイヴ,,Biyondo Za Gureivu?), nicknamed the"Gunslinger of Resurrection"and the"Grim Reaper", is a legendaryDeadmangunslinger,  the reincarnation ofBrandon Heat, and the main protagonist and anti-hero of theGungraveseries. 46 (Gungrave: Overdose)59 (Gungrave VR)Unknown (Gungrave: G.O.R.E) Black (Gungrave G.O.R.E) In the game, Grave is an extremely silent person, even more than when he was human. Despite being almost incapable of feeling, he proves to still be his kind self to his friends. For the most part, it's true that he's more often than not; a stoic, silent and cold character. However, his horrid and unfortunate past and him having a hard time trying to remember anything at all - it's easily excusable. His "get the job done" and cold demeanor sort of gives him that "cold character" aura, however, it's true; he's cold and for the most part, emotionless; his fight with Bunji in the original Gungrave and Overdose is a little proof of it, with Bunji giving a monologue while Brandon just's there, standing still[5]; however, it makes him quite unpredictable a little. His silent and cold personality gives him an advantage-yet-a-disadvantage. However, it can be accurately stated that Grave does not care at all about someones fate; usually, someone he fought and would kill them ruthlessly. Dr. T described Grave as Millenions "worst nightmare", and all of their members would go to their graves; every last one of them.[6]Despite this, he's a loyal and caring friend;Mika Asagi, the daughter of Big Daddy was in-danger, however, Grave would do anything to protect her[7]- albeit, usually just nodding when he's ordered to do something.[8]Despite this, his cold demeanor could mean that he's still suffering from amnesia[9], as he's literally just brought back; and is only awakened when needed, therefore; he has a limited time exploring the world, as his body constantly needs to hiberna

characters.name: Brandon Heat
characters.description: Brandon HeatGameYoung (Anime)1st Timeskip (Anime)2nd Timeskip (Anime)NameKanjiブランドン・ヒートRomanjiBurandon HītoEnglishBrandon HeatProfileGenderMaleAge18 (in street gang)24 (first timeskip)27 (second timeskip)SpeciesHumanOccupationSweeperOther InformationHair ColorBlackEye ColorBrownWeaponsPistolsTwin Sig Sauer P226'sSniper RifleStatusStatusDeceased (necrolyzed intoBeyond the Grave)Brandon Heat(ブランドン・ヒート,,Burandon Hīto?)was the human identity ofBeyond the Grave. Once a member of a small-time street gang, after meetingBear Walken, he decides to joinMillennionwith his best friendHarry McDowell, becoming an expert sweeper and friends with the organization's leaderBig Daddy. Brandon is a calm, kind and loyal man despite being raised in the streets. He cares more for those around him than himself: he refused to leave his gang and find a job with Maria's uncle so that he could continue to fight for his friends, going as far as killing Cid Gallarde for Bear Walken as part of the Code of Iron when the latter was unable to do so, and later willingly gave up Maria to Big Daddy to ensure her safety. His kindness also extends to as many people as he can help, or at least keep away from the crossfire: Brandon was adamant about isolating targets of Millennion from their families before performing the hit, a fact his subordinates brought up shortly before Bunji decided to slaughter a target and his family. Prior to this incident, when Bunji injured himself on a hit, Brandon silently berated Bunji's carelessness with a slap across the face, and then told him to get his injuries checked. Prior to this incident, when Bunji injured himself on a hit, Brandon silently berated Bunji's carelessness with a slap across the face, and then told him to get his injuries checked. Brandon is also a person of very few words, instead letting his actions speak for themselves. characters.name: Big Daddy
characters.description: Big DaddyGameMutatedAnimeAlias(es)Caldwell AsagiThe BossBiologicalGenderMaleAge60+Hair ColorDark GreyEye ColorBrownOther InfoFamilyMaria Asagi(Wife, Deceased)Mika Asagi(Daughter)Mr. Tokioka(Butler, Deceased)Bear Walken(Former Right-hand man, Deceased)StatusDeceasedVoice ActorsJapanese VAIemasa KayumiEnglish VAMichael McConnohie Mika Asagi(Daughter)Mr. Tokioka(Butler, Deceased)Bear Walken(Former Right-hand man, Deceased) Caldwell Asagi(コールドウェルアサギ,Kōrudou Aruasagi?), better known asBig Daddy(ビッグ・ダディ,Biggu Dadi?), is the founder and former boss ofMillennion, an organized crime group, andMika's late father. He is also the first part of the final boss ofGungravefollowed up by Alien Head. Although Big Daddy had committed many crimes in the name of the organization, he is not a bloodthirsty man, and in fact, is rather kind and paternal – almost like a grandfather. However, Big Daddy is also systematic and follows the Code of Iron as much as his followers do, albeit regretfully: upon realizing long time friendCid Gallarde's betrayal, Big Daddy admits to Maria that he had to make many difficult decisions and begins drinking in the difficulty of having to order Cid's execution. Bear Walken also describes Big Daddy as "a little awkward", primarily for putting his work before all else. While he does have feelings for Maria, Big Daddy is more supportive of her relationship with Brandon, at one point setting up Brandon to meet with Maria. Brandon puts Maria's safety and his loyalty to Millennion first, however, and reveals to her fully his job as a hitman, enabling Big Daddy to enter into a relationship with his beloved.

characters.name: Blood War
characters.description: Blood WarAliases/NicknamesEnglishBlood of the BattlefieldProfileGenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationMafia Member(formerly)Other InformationHair ColorRedWeaponsSilver and Gold Customized Ruger Super RedhawksRelationshipsFriendsCannon VulcanAffiliatesVolcano(formerly)EnemiesMillennionBrandon HeatHarry MacDowellBunji KugashiraStatusStatusDeceasedVoice ActorsJapanese VAMasahiko TanakaDub VATaylor Henry(English) Blood Waris a member of Lightning, a rival gang toMillennion, and ex-high ranking-member of Volcano, another mafia organization. War is comrade-in-arms toCannon Vulcan. Nicknamed "Blood of the Battlefield", War is a hotheaded and belligerent man, relishing fighting for the sake of fighting. War is also incredibly ruthless, shooting one of his underlings for prioritizing his family to his job as a hitman, although he had done so after witnessingDr. Tokioka's necrolization research and was going to prepare the man for necrolization anyway. After witnessingBrandon Heatpointing a gun at him to protectHarry McDowell, War takes a liking to Brandon, calling him "little fella" and asks him to join him and his gang and betrayBig Daddy. Even when Brandon refuses, War does not mind, relishing the gunfight between his men and Brandon instead. characters.name: Bob Poundmax
characters.description: Bob PoundmaxNameKanjiボブ・パウンドマックスRomanjiBobu PaundomakkusuProfileGenderMaleSpeciesSuperiorOccupationChief of IntelligenceMember of the Family's Big FourOther InformationHair ColorBlondeEye ColorBlackRelationshipsFriendsHarry MacDowellBalladbird LeeBear WalkenBunji KugashiraBrandon Heat(Formerly)EnemiesBeyond the GraveMika AsagiDr. TStatusStatusDeceased Bob Poundmax(ボブ・パウンドマックス,Bobu Paundomakkusu?)was one of the Big Four inMillennion's the Family, and the chief of intelligence in the organization, a major antagonist and boss character in theoriginalGungravegame, and theanime adaption. In the original game, Bob appears as a considerably overweight man with a short and small nose, pronounced jowls and no eyebrows. His hair is styled into a classic short pompadour. Through the first half of the fight, he wears a long purple overcoat overtop his traditional black and white tuxedo that's adorned by a red bowtie. During his early days in Millennion, Bob was a jovial character, hardly seen without food, especially fried chicken, and willingly performing intelligence operations for Harry as needed.Bob expressed worry and concern for Harry's well-being when contacting Brandon while Harry was planning on recruiting Bunji Kugashira. Still, there were some things that Bob did not compromise on, as he never disclosed his contact list to anybody, not even his best friend Lee. As a Superior, Bob has a more obnoxious personality, taking a bite out of his drumstick and then throwing it in Grave's direction in their first meeting in thirteen years. He expresses disgust that Grave would return to destroy Millennion even after all that the organization has done for him. He stops Bunji from firing at his former mentor, but out of eagerness to test his new powers as a Superior rather than any amount of goodwill towards Grave.

characters.name: Bunji Kugashira
characters.description: Bunji KugashiraG.O.R.EOverdoseGungraveNameRomanji句が知ら 文治EnglishBunji KugashiraAliases/NicknamesJapaneseMadness Bunji(マッドネス 文治,,Maddonesu Bunji?)The WolfEnglishMadman BunjiThe WolfProfileGenderMaleAgeAround 36 (2nd timeskip, anime)SpeciesPartSuperior(Original game & anime)DeadmanOther InformationHair ColorBlackEye ColorBlack or BrownWeaponsBrowning Hi Power with 30 Round drum magsHeckler and Koch USPRelationshipsFamilyJyuji Kabane(Younger brother)StatusStatusAliveBunji Kugashira(句が知ら 文治,,Kugashira Bunji?)is a highly skilled gunman,Grave's rival, and a recurring character in theGungraveseries. Heckler and Koch USP He was one of the Big Four ofMillennion's Family, a legendary hitman, previously nicknamed; "The Wolf", however, was eventually indirectly however, killed by Grave as he did not land the final blow. Because of his excellent skills, andBrandon's apprentice and good friend until after Brandon's death, believing he had betrayed Millennion. InGungrave: Overdose, after the collapse of Millenion and the deaths of all its top brass on the airship including Bunji himself in the first game thanks to Grave, he was resurrected by theCorsione Familyas aDeadmanand is recruited as a sweeper. However, he is killed a second time in a duel by the hands of either Grave, again,Jyuji KabaneorRocketbilly Redcadillac. Bunji has a strange sense of morals in life. Despite killing many he is extremely loyal to those he works for, and after joining Millennion, blindly follows the Code of Iron after Brandon's example. For this reason, he considers himself Brandon's first apprentice and presumably looked up to Brandon as the embodiment of the Code of Iron. Naturally, despite initially acting nonchalant about Brandon's betrayal, Bunji took the news harshly, killing off a Millennion target and his family out of disdain for his late mentor's comparatively peaceful ways of refusing the target's family to be involved and shortly after renaming True Grave to the Kugashira Group. After Bob Poundmax's death, Bunji even goes out of his way to finish off Grave personally, independent of and disregarding Lee's simultaneous desire for revenge. Bunji's version of the Code of Iron differs from Brandon's only by loyalty: Bunji's being more focused on Harry than on the rest of Millennion, likely due to his respect for Harry. He executed Randy because while the latter had not betrayed Millennion, he had "betrayed Harry MacDowell". Thirteen years after Harry's ascension, Bunji has a habit of killing off the people he works with indiscriminately, considering them useless after Millennion's employee base has expanded so much at the expense of quality, and prefers to work alone. Bunji also holdsLaguna Glockin disdain for the scientist's eagerness in pursuing Necrolization, but solicits his services in order to gain power to match Grave. In the anime, after his first defeat by Grave, Bunji regains respect for his mentor and even adopts his signature finger taunt to start the battle. InOv

characters.description: Cannon VulcanProfileGenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationMafia Member(Lightning)Other InformationHair ColorBlackEye ColorBrownRelationshipsFamilyBalladbird Lee(Younger Brother)FriendsBlood WarAffiliatesLightningEnemiesMillennionBig DaddyHarry MacDowellBrandon HeatStatusStatusDeceasedVoice ActorsJapanese VATakeru MiyashitaDub VAKurt Strauss(English) Big DaddyHarry MacDowellBrandon Heat Cannon Vulcanis a high-ranking member of Lightning, a rival gang toMillennion. Vulcan isBalladbird Lee's brother and comrade-in-arms toBlood War. In contrast with his close friend Blood War, Vulcan is calm and calculating, but equally ruthless and belligerent. Vulcan had originally killed his father, coldly dismissing it as a past incident when Lee brought it up in their adulthood. He started the Raven Clan shortly after to distribute SEED, in which; he aims to spread SEED throughout the entire world, and possibly become one of the strongest, ever.

characters.name: Garino Creale Corsione
characters.description: Garino Creale CorsioneDebutGungrave: OverdoseBiologicalSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHair ColorBlondeEye ColorBlueOther InfoAffiliationCorsione FamilyOccupationBoss (After betraying Don Corsione)Head of Research Department (Formerly)FamilyDon Denito Corsione(Adoptive father)Affilliate(s)Don Denito Corsione(Superior; formerly)Fangoram(Bodyguard & subordinate)Sherry MacDowell-Walken(Subordinate)Zell Condorbrave(Subordinate)Bunji Kugashira(Subordinate)Enemy(s)Beyond the GraveJuji KabaneRocketbilly RedcadillacMika AsagiSpike HubieGatou Gang(Competitors)StatusDeceasedVoice ActorsEnglish VARobert Atkins Downes Garino Creale Corsione(ガリノ・クレアーレ・コルシオネ,,Garino Kureāre Korushiōne?)was the head of theCorsione Family's research department (later boss of the organization), having remade theOrgmento use in battle,Don Corsione's adopted son, and is the primary antagonist inGungrave: Overdose. Garino looks like an average man; with a pale skin, fluffy blonde hair, and blue eyes. He has a normal build, and appears to be in his 20s or some sort. His usual attire consists of a white shirt with a red tie, covered by a blue suit with a matching blue dress pants and finished with a pair of black business shoes. To put it simply, he looks like an average business man. Garino is a black-hearted, cool, calm & collected man, always on track & rarely stressed. Near the end of the game, he shows his true colors, yet begins to descend into madness. He reveals that the game's events happened because he was bored, having already obtained everything he could ever want. Thinking that he's practically god, he felt like he has nothing to strive for. And because that there was nothing left on earth for him to do, he made a deal with the alien raceSEEDand he intended to explore the galaxy due to his boredom. Ironically, his personality is everything that his clone,Spike Hubieisn't. He's just borderline psychopathic, however, intelligent. To be precise, he was the one who was behind everything that led to Overdose's events, even his father was surprised and called him an ingrate. His borderline, psychopathic nature was shown when he laughed manically about Spike's death, which, even Grave was angered by his actions; and Grave was shown to be quite calm and cool, just like him.

characters.name: Gary and Widge
characters.description: Gary and Widgeare a pair of men who run a horse-race betting track underMillennionin the anime series. The two wereBrandon Heat's first coworkers in the organization before the young man became a sweeper, and years later take care ofMika Asagiwhen Grave leaves her in their care. Of the duo, Gary is the more impulsive and hotheaded of the duo, while Widge, the older of the two and the manager of the betting center, is more calm and rational. During the young Brandon's employment at the betting center, Gary would frequently lash out at the young man for making mistakes, only to be calmed down by Widge. Gary is also an alcoholic, and would frequently be seen with a bottle of alcohol.

His ambition and desire for freedom led him to betray and kill Brandon in his quest to become head of the Millennion. As the leader of the crime syndicate, he consolidates his power through ruthless and unethical tactics. Harry's sins and new responsibilities as the boss of Millennion seem to take a toll on his health, as thirteen years after he becomes the boss, though still very capable, he appears as an old man with gray hair, despite only being in his early forties. From childhood, Harry MacDowell has desired freedom above all else. He's extremely charismatic and clever but also impatient and prone to taking shortcuts in order to get ahead. While initially satisfied with the free life that being a street thug afforded him, the tragic losses of Jolice, Kenny, and Nathan would prompt Harry to seek to get ahead by any means necessary, to defend his freedom with power. In Harry's own words, he wanted the power "to give as [he wants] and to take as [he wants]". Harry's ruthlessness is also exemplified by his usage of his gun. When presented with a target, Harry shoots the target multiple times even after the target has already fallen or died. In the game, the younger Harry exhibits a cooler personality, but just as short a temper: killing Brandon in the elevator for punching him and elaborately telling his corpse to "fall, down into the darkness which suits [his] grave so well". After his strongest units are defeated, Harry graciously allows Brandon to kill him without putting up any resistance. Ambition and lust for power aside, Harry holds a place in his heart for his best friend Brandon, even after Harry's betrayal and their subsequent conflict. characters.name: Jester
characters.description: Jester JesterwasMaria Asagi's adoptive uncle and her caretaker for the majority of her youth. Jester had worked as a sweeper ofMillennionand was close toBig Daddy. On one hit, however, he had killed the parents of the infant Maria and out of remorse, took Maria in and swore off his job in the organization, making a peaceful living instead. Big Daddy's high regard for Jester even after the nearly two decades that followed implies that when Jester quit being a sweeper, they parted on amicable terms;Bear Walkenadds that Big Daddy would not be happy about his death upon witnessing said death byMad Dog Ladd.

characters.name: Jyuji Kabane
characters.description: Jyuji KabaneProfileGenderMaleAgeUnknownSpeciesHalf-Deadman/Half-SEEDOccupationDrifter (currently)Mafia Member (assumed, formally)Other InformationHair ColorWhiteEye ColorWhiteWeaponsTsumji and Hayate (Gunblades)RelationshipsFamilyBunji Kugashira(Older Brother)FriendsRocketbilly RedcadillacBeyond the GraveMika AsagiSpike HubieAlliesGatou GangEnemiesCorsione FamilyStatusStatusAlive [Deadman]Voice ActorsJapanese VAHiroaki HirataDub VACam Clark(English Dub)Jyuji Kabane(カバネ・十次,,Kabane Jūji?)formally known asJyuji Kugashira(句が知ら・十次,,Kugashira Jūji?)is a deadman drifter as well as partner toBilly, despite having seemingly opposite personalities. He is one of the protagonistsandplayable characters inGungrave: Overdose. Jyuji's voice actors are Cam Clark in the English dub and Hiroaki Hirata in the Japanese dub. Mafia Member (assumed, formally) Jyuji has a pale complexion accompanied by short, choppy white hair. On his face he has scruffy facial hair on his chin as well as multiple stitches; one going from the right side of his mouth down to the bottom of his chin and another running diagonally across his face from his right cheek and stopping around the top of his nose bridge. When he takes off his signature brown eye mask later on in the story, it’s revealed that his eyes are completely whited over by SEED which is what caused him to go completely blind. The surrounding area of his eyes, and slightly down his cheeks to his jaw is covered in similarly colored veins. "Y’know Juji, it seems to me you have a problem with that big mouth of yours. That’s why you’re not exactly Mr. Popularity." - Billy Jyuji has a very abrasive personality. He is very straightforward, with both his feelings and how he fights. Billy laments that the only plan he ever comes up with is to rush in and do a frontal assault, showing that Jyuji is confident in his power and doesn’t care to sit back and think but instead get to the action. Despite this, he has a very high intelligence and often doesn’t act overly recklessly. He doesn’t like dealing with weak people, especially children, and often laments the fact he is stuck ‘babysitting’ Spike and Mika. There are many points in which he talks very harshly towards them, believing they’re caught up in something way over their heads. Spike gets the worst of this treatment, as the two are constantly seen bickering back and forth. He instinctively doesn’t trust Spike, his sharp nose telling him that something was ‘off’ with him. He does secretly have a sense of humor however, cracking jokes on the rare occasion, and isn’t a completely unreasonable man. Despite the fact he seems to blow up with anger very often, his voice is always level. This could be due to the fact that if he loses control of his emotions, the SEED in his body could also lose control and take over. Towards the end of the game, he is noticeably kinder. characters.description: Jyuji KabaneProfileGenderMaleAgeUnknownSpeciesHalf-Deadman/Half-SEEDOccupationDrifter (currently)Mafia Member (assumed, formally)Other InformationHair ColorWhiteEye ColorWhiteWeaponsTsumji and Hayate (Gunblades)RelationshipsFamilyBunji Kugashira(Older Brother)FriendsRocketbilly RedcadillacBeyond the GraveMika AsagiSpike HubieAlliesGatou GangEnemiesCorsione FamilyStatusStatusAlive [Deadman]Voice ActorsJapanese VAHiroaki HirataDub VACam Clark(English Dub)Jyuji Kabane(カバネ・十次,,Kabane Jūji?)formally known asJyuji Kugashira(句が知ら・十次,,Kugashira Jūji?)is a deadman drifter as well as partner toBilly, despite having seemingly opposite personalities. He is one of the protagonistsandplayable characters inGungrave: Overdose. Jyuji's voice actors are Cam Clark in the English dub and Hiroaki Hirata in the Japanese dub. Mafia Member (assumed, formally) Jyuji has a pale complexion accompanied by short, choppy white hair. On his face he has scruffy facial hair on his chin as well as multiple stitches; one going from the right side of his mouth down to the bottom of his chin and another running diagonally across his face from his right cheek and stopping around the top of his nose bridge. When he takes off his signature brown eye mask later on in the story, it’s revealed that his eyes are completely whited over by SEED which is what caused him to go completely blind. The surrounding area of his eyes, and slightly down his cheeks to his jaw is covered in similarly colored veins. "Y’know Juji, it seems to me you have a problem with that big mouth of yours. That’s why you’re not exactly Mr. Popularity." - Billy Jyuji has a very abrasive personality. He is very straightforward, with both his feelings and how he fights. Billy laments that the only plan he ever comes up with is to rush in and do a frontal assault, showing that Jyuji is confident in his power and doesn’t care to sit back and think but instead get to the action. Despite this, he has a very high intelligence and often doesn’t act overly recklessly. He doesn’t like dealing with weak people, especially children, and often laments the fact he is stuck ‘babysitting’ Spike and Mika. There are many points in which he talks very harshly towards them, believing they’re caught up in something way over their heads. Spike gets the worst of this treatment, as the two are constantly seen bickering back and forth. He instinctively doesn’t trust Spike, his sharp nose telling him that something was ‘off’ with him. He does secretly have a sense of humor however, cracking jokes on the rare occasion, and isn’t a completely unreasonable man. Despite the fact he seems to blow up with anger very often, his voice is always level. This could be due to the fact that if he loses control of his emotions, the SEED in his body could also lose control and take over. Towards the end of the game, he is noticeably kinder. The main group had earned his trust, which was an impressive feat considering Jyuji has intense trust issues due to his

characters.name: Laguna Glock
characters.description: Dr. Laguna Glock(ラグナー・グロック,,Raguna Gurokku?)isMillennion's head scientist underHarry MacDowell, and the former assistant ofDr. T. Glock invented the process of Superiorization, enabling Harry's Big Four to become Superiors. Unlike Dr. T, who regrets his role in fathering the science of Necrolization, Dr. Glock has no qualms with the science and wholeheartedly pushes it as far as it can go. Glock even watches gleefully as the prototypeOrgmenravage Dr. T's lab shortly before his former head's disappearance, and the morning after the lab is destroyed, approaches Harry and proposes the idea of Superiorization. Thirteen years after the incident, Glock has not changed in demeanor at all, and even admires the Orgmen he had produced in his test chambers. Glock also seems to suffer from an inferiority complex towards Dr. T: when Grave ravages his lab, an angry and horrified Glock stands aside and wonders if the destruction of his lab signifies Dr. T's superiority. characters.name: Mad Dog Ladd
characters.description: Mad Dog Laddis a former street thug in the town thatBrandon HeatandHarry McDowellgrew up in. Mad Dog has a weaker younger brother namedDeed, who frequently got into fights with Brandon and Harry's gang before their deaths. At some point prior to the start of the story, Mad Dog left the town he had grown up in for unknown reasons. Harry speculates that Mad Dog had died, but in fact, Mad Dog had joined Lightning and returned as a member of the mafia. Angry at his younger brother Deed's weakness against Harry's gang and inability to fire a real gun to kill, especially as it also gives him a bad name, Mad Dog walks to Harry's hangout, but finding nobody there butJolice, kills the cook with a single shot to the head. Harry, Brandon, Kenny, and Nathan later return to discover Jolice's corpse, and are forced into hiding as a result of Mad Dog's violence. Later, Mad Dog walks into a laboratory owned byMillennionand kills all the scientists there, sparing onlyDr. Tokioka, who he kidnaps to work for Lightning.

characters.name: Mika Asagi
characters.description: Mika AsagiAnimeOverdoseGOREAliases/NicknamesEnglish"Boss"(by Quartz)ProfileGenderFemaleAge14 (Game)/13 (Anime)17 (Gungrave: Overdose)30 (Gungrave VR)Unknown (Gungrave: G.O.R.E)SpeciesSEEDOccupationLeader of theEl-Al CanhelOther InformationHair ColorBlack-to-silver gradientEye ColorAqua Blue/TealWeaponsSmith & Wesson Model 60RelationshipsFamilyBeyond the Grave(Adoptive father)Maria Asagi(Mother; deceased)Big Daddy(Father; deceased)FriendsDr. TJuji KabaneRocketbilly RedcadillacSpike HubieQuartzDr. AsoAffiliatesBeyond the Grave(Subordinate)Bunji Kugashira(Subordinate)Quartz(Subordinate)Dr. Aso(Subordinate)Zell Condorbrave(Subordinate)AlliesBeyond the GraveDr. TJuji KabaneRocketbilly RedcadillacSpike HubieGatou GangEnemiesMillenionunderHarry MacDowell's leadershipCorsione FamilyRaven ClanStatusStatusAliveMika Asagi(みか 浅葱,,Mika Asagi?)isGrave's companion and adopted daughter, the biological daughter of the lateMaria AsagiandBig Daddy, the later being the former head ofMillennion, and the deuteragonist of theGungraveseries. Raven Clan Throughout thefirst gameandanime series, she goes through many hardships as she sees those close to her pass away one by one, killed by Millennion's underlings. In between the first game andGungrave: Overdose, Mika has spent 3 years dedicating herself into battling the issues created by the Necrolyzation process, leading to into conflict withSEEDand  theCorsione Family. During this, she protected Grave while he's been hibernating in his coffin until he awakens once again to join in the fight. InGungrave G.O.R.E, Mika is the leader of theEl-Al Canhel, an organization dedicated to eliminating all SEED in the world and its suppliers, theRaven Clan. Mika's personality resembles Maria's: kind, gentle, and strong-willed. Due to her mother's death, she struggles between her revenge towardHarry MacDowelland her pain of losing more and more of her loved ones by Millennion. In the game, Mika met Harry once when she was young. Harry appeared to be fond of Mika and Mika does not understand why he is trying to kill her. In the anime, due to her closer involvement with the other characters, Mika is more vocal and expressive about protecting Grave, shouting at Bunji or Biscoe to stop hurting him and begging Grave not to go out and wear out his gradually-deteriorating body; in episode 24, even confessing that she loves him. Mika had even told Grave previously that she could understand why her mother loved him and admonished him for being unresponsive to a girl like her.

characters.name: Rocketbilly Redcadillac
characters.description: Rocketbilly RedcadillacNameKanjiロケットビリー・レッドキャデラックRomanjiRokettobirī ReddokyaderakkuEnglishRocketbilly RedcadillacProfileGenderMaleAgeUnknownSpeciesHumanOccupationDrifterRockstarOther InformationHair ColorBlondeEye ColorBlueWeaponsBL20000V (Electric Guitar)RelationshipsFriendsJyuji KabaneBeyond the GraveMika AsagiSpike HubieAlliesGatou GangEnemiesCorsione FamilyStatusStatusAlive [Ghost]Rocketbilly Redcadillac(ロケットビリー・レッドキャデラック,,Rokettobirī Reddokyaderakku?),"Billy"or"RB"(by Jyuji) is a ghost haunting an electric guitar, partner toJyujiwhom had found and saved him sometime during his travels. He is one the protagonistsandplayable characters inGungrave: Overdose. Billy's voice actors are Chis Cook in the English dub and Hochu Otsuka in the Japanese dub. Rockstar Beyond the GraveMika AsagiSpike Hubie “Yeah, I told you that getup of yours was too flashy.” - Jyuji Billy has a very bright personality. Laid-back, charming and funny, he often gets along with anyone and everyone including enemies. He very rarely ever takes charge unless it’s to de-escalate a situation, preferring to go with the flow of things and take what life throws at him. He’s especially kind to women, being called out by Jyuji for constantly chasing women and being a ladies' man. It’s shown he expresses his emotions through his music, either playing upbeat tunes or sad requiems depending on the situation. Bizarrely, he considers his own grandmother to be the ideal woman.
